parameters: Journalist: "Mr. Stephen Steel, about the 'Steel Ball Run'
race that begins in two days. You have explained things
before, but for the readers around the world, let us move
on."
cam flash sound
Steel: "The goal will be New York! Making it mankind's first
American cross-country horseback race!"
"The champion will receive Fifty Million American Dollars,
which will be deposited into the individual's East and West
bank accounts!"
"As for the runner-ups, 2nd place: receive $1 million,
"And so on, continuing through 10th place."
"Please read the rulebook for more details."
"As long as the participant is age 16 or older, nationality,
sex, race, and pro or amateur status wil not be questioned.
There is a necessary participation fee, which is $1,200 per
person."
Journalist: "Tell us about the sponsors of this contest."
Aide: "First of all, your news company, East & West
Tribunal, has exclusive coverage of this race. Others
include the respective publishing companies, hotel groups
including the beach hotel, B&C Packaging Corporation..."
"...Winchester Firearm Production. Winchester Firearm
Productionn, Horizontal Continental Railroad Corporation,
Speedwagon Oil Corporation, and many others across America."
Journalist: "Mr. Steel, I believe Baron Rockahugo of German
Royalty has announced participation in machine called called
an 'automobile'?"
Steel: "Ah, yes, I approved of that. The race's identity is
the pioneering spirit! When the railroads were first opened
in North America, people said this:
Steel: 'The age of the horse is over.'\FS[28]
This race accepts that challenge. Come with any kind of
ground transporation, machine or horse!"
"This race is a first in history! Nobody will be able to
predict anything that happens! With one machine, or with one
horse, nobody has ever raced without rest, with competition
for over 50 miles a day, for a total of 3500 miles!"
Journalist: "Is that estimation a limit to how far the horses
can run? That means it woul dtake roughly 60 to 80 days to
each the goal."
Steel: "Well, there was a record of quaterhorses running
1800 miles between Missouri and California in 12 days, but
that was a relay of 400 horses."
"And during the Crimean War, an Arabian horse ran 90 miles in
a day, but he rider died of exhaustion."
"In this race, in spite of the environmental conditions of
the wastelands and uncharted territories, the exchange of
horses is prohibited and willresult in disqualification."
Journalist: "There are some complaints about the fee. It's
roughly the same as a worker's monthly salary."
Journalist: "There are some complaints about the fee. It's
roughly the same as a worker's monthly salary."
Steel: "I don't see the issue. We will cover their travel
expenses, medical expenses, and food expenses in the case
of dropping out."
Steel: "I don't see the issue. We will cover their travel
expenses, medical expenses, and food expenses in the case
of dropping out."
Steel: "Yes, we will not infringe on one's 2nd amendment
rights, however weapons are to be used strictly for
protection purposes. It should go without saying what will
happen to racers who use their arms with criminal intent."
Journalist: "Tell us some of the champion candidates for this
race."
Prelude 3: Mountain Tim
Dot Han
Avdol
Diego Brando
Journalist: "The distance is tremendous... If nobody reaches
the finish line, the sponsors will doubtlessly be furious!
All the trust you've earned will be lost! How would you
take responsibility for that?"
Steel: "...I might have to pay for that... with my life."
Shock emotes from everyone in varying orders
Lucy and Aide turn
Steel: "...That was a joke."
Journalist: "...Ha, ha... Ha..."
Steel: "Failure is- Listen closely! True failure is...!
Forgetting the pioneer spirit! And avoiding challenging
hardships!"
"There is no such thing as failure in this race! There is
only discovery and adventure!"
"This 'Steel Ball Run' race will be an event the likes of
which this world has never experienced before!"
Steel, Lucy, and Aide begin to leave
Young Journalist: "Um! One more question Mr. Steel!"
"Is the person behind you your wife? I've heard she's 14..."
Steel: "...That has nothing to do with this race."
Aide: "No more questions please! The interview session is
over!"
